title: fix: strip whitespace from Vectorize/Pollinations secret env vars
author: Itachi-1824
pr_body_excerpt:
Two fixes surfaced by the first manual full-backfill run, both in the same script:

**1. Secret whitespace.** First run failed immediately with `requests.exceptions.InvalidHeader` — one of the CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID/CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN/POLLI_VECTOR_DB secrets had trailing whitespace. `requests` rejects that outright as a header value. Now stripped defensively.

**2. Vector ID length.** After the whitespace fix, the run got to file 25/1852 then failed with `id too long; max is 64 bytes, got 75 bytes`. The `path:start-end` id scheme doesn't survive this repo's real path depth (paths under `enter.pollinations.ai/frontend/src/components/...` are already close to 64 bytes before line numbers). Now hashing the identifier instead — deterministic (incremental delete/re-insert by id still works), fixed-length, and `file_path`/`start_line`/`end_line` stay in metadata for readable query results.

gist.headline: Backfill Gets Its Bearings
gist.blurb: The code-indexing garden now trims stray secret whitespace before getting to work, and gives every chunk a compact hashed tag. Deep paths can keep growing without upsetting the vector shelf.
gist.summary: Updated `.github/scripts/embed_code.py` to strip whitespace from Vectorize/Pollinations secret environment variables and replace long `path:start-end` vector IDs with deterministic fixed-length hashes, while retaining file path and line metadata.
gist.impact: Full code backfills can now proceed past whitespace-tainted credentials and deeply nested repository paths without invalid-header or 64-byte vector-ID failures.
gist.visual_concept: Polly tends a cozy code garden beside a retro terminal, carefully trimming a trailing bit of whitespace from a secret-label ribbon while compact seed-shaped hash tokens are filed into a vector archive. Deep code-vines represent long repository paths, with flowers and readable file-and-line labels showing that useful metadata remains available.

Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, with subtle CRT glow and dark purple (#110518) outlines.
